data.title: Trump stands by tariffs despite Supreme Court ruling. US economy grew 1.4% in Q4, down from 4.4%. Inflation at 3%. SPY stock hits highs.
data.description: The US economy expanded at a 1.4% annualized pace in the fourth quarter, a significant slowdown from the prior quarter's 4.4% growth and below expectations of 3%. The Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge rose to a 3% annual rate in December. President Trump attributed the weak growth to the government shutdown, estimating it reduced GDP by 2%. The Supreme Court made a landmark decision revoking the administration's tariff authority under the IEEPA, asserting that only Congress can impose tariffs. State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust stock is trading near recent highs. Trump also blamed tariffs for the growth slowdown.

data.title: Surge Copper's Berg Project accepted into BC Critical Minerals Office, Feb 20, 2026. TSXV: SURG, OTCQB: SRGXF, Frankfurt: G6D2.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia on February 20, 2026, Surge Copper Corp. shared that the Berg Project has been accepted into the Critical Minerals Office by the British Columbia government. Surge Copper Corp. trades on the TSXV as SURG, on the OTCQB as SRGXF, and on the Frankfurt exchange as G6D2. This move signifies the official recognition of the project's importance in the critical minerals sector.

data.title: Century Aluminum Company response to US Supreme Court IEEPA decision on tariffs. (NASDAQ: CENX) issued a statement on Feb 20, 2026.
data.description: In Chicago on February 20, 2026, Century Aluminum Company (NASDAQ: CENX) released a statement concerning the recent U.S. Supreme Court ruling on tariffs enacted pursuant to the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA).

data.title: FaceTec aims to prevent $30B+ healthcare identity fraud using UR® Codes to verify patient identities.
data.description: FaceTec introduces UR® Codes technology in SUMMERLIN, Nevada on Feb. 20, 2026. This innovation combines humans with their verified identities through various documents, ensuring robust patient matching and preventing entitlement fraud and deceitful service provision. FaceTec, known for its pioneering 3D Face Verification software, spearheads this development.

data.title: Matt Shaw reappointed as Vice President of Sales at Mighty Auto Parts, to drive sales strategy for product lines & revenue growth.
data.description: In Peachtree Corners, Georgia on February 20, 2026, Mighty Auto Parts made public that Matt Shaw has been reinstated as the Vice President of Sales. In this position, Shaw will spearhead sales strategy and implementation to drive the rapid growth of essential product lines and revenue within the Mighty system.

data.title: Graphic Packaging Holding Company will present at Raymond James Institutional Investors Conference on March 4. CEO Robbert Rietbroek to speak. $GPK #packaging
data.description: Atlanta, GA - On February 20, 2026, Graphic Packaging Holding Company (NYSE: GPK) shared an update on their leadership team. Robbert Rietbroek, the President and Chief Executive Officer, along with Charles "Chuck" Lischer, the Senior Vice President, were mentioned in the announcement. Graphic Packaging is recognized as a key player in sustainable consumer packaging globally.

data.title: CUPE Nova Scotia urges Premier Houston to support long term care workers with fair deals instead of benefiting private firms.
data.description: In Halifax, Nova Scotia, the President of CUPE Nova Scotia, Alan Linkletter, has addressed a letter to Premier Houston urging the conservative government to address the concerns of long term care workers. Linkletter criticized the government for prioritizing private companies over workers, citing examples of millions of dollars being allocated to contracts with private entities instead of investing in fair compensation for the workers. Since Houston took office, there have been instances of the government favoring private companies with financial incentives instead of supporting the labor force.

data.title: Eric Trump supports World Liberty's plan for a Maldives hotel tokenization project as an exit strategy against meme coins, citing a lengthy timeline.
data.description: Eric Trump described the offering as a counter to meme coins, emphasizing the tokenization project's extended duration.

data.title: Potential $600M in Bitcoin liquidation puts bears at risk, increasing odds for push to $70K amid improving hashrate and security measures.
data.description: Even with downward pressure and poor US economic indicators, Bitcoin has seen an increase in its hashrate and implementation of new security protocols on the blockchain. This raises optimism for a potential rally towards the $70,000 mark.

data.title: Dutch authorities urge Polymarket's division to halt operations amid allegations of illegal bets on Dutch elections.
data.description: Allegations have surfaced regarding Adventure One, a division of the prediction market Dutch arm, for reportedly facilitating illegal wagers. These illicit bets supposedly encompassed various events, including elections within the Netherlands.

data.title: Proforce Pest Control teams up with OutboundIQ to boost customer engagement and safeguard revenue.
data.description: A partnership has been revealed between Proforce Pest Control, a prominent provider of pest management services in the Southeast, and OutboundIQ on Feb. 20, 2026. This strategic collaboration aims to boost customer engagement and ensure consistent revenue. Based in Boca Raton, Florida, Proforce Pest Control offers services for both residential and commercial properties. The company’s partnership with OutboundIQ signifies a step towards expanding their outreach efforts.

data.title: Cooper Standard prices $1.1B of Senior Secured First Lien Notes through its subsidiary Cooper-Standard Automotive Inc.
data.description: On February 20, 2026, in Northville, Michigan, Cooper-Standard Holdings Inc. (NYSE: CPS) disclosed the pricing details of a private offering led by its wholly-owned subsidiary, Cooper-Standard Automotive Inc. The offering amounted to $1.1 billion.

data.title: Article discusses personal care services for adults with disabilities in Ohio. #HighlightingIndependentLiving #HomeHealthcareExpert #CantonOhio
data.description: In Canton, Ohio, on February 20, 2026, a recent article provides guidance on utilizing daily living assistance and family support to enhance independent living for adults with disabilities. It offers key insights for families seeking homemaker services and personal care services for adult relatives with disabilities. This guidance aims to facilitate independence and foster a supportive environment for individuals needing care. The article emphasizes the importance of access to these services to ensure the well-being and autonomy of adults with disabilities.
